generatedSentence: Under the streaked night, a vulnerable grandfather, peering through his eyeglass, watched vicariously as a hacky coalition, planning to upcycle a drainpipe by the mucky waterway, organized an overnight jubilee to demand their missing payroll from the elites who lavished themselves with omakase, mustards, and buttercup kernels, oblivious to the newer waves of unrest. This preliminary kickoff was anything but boring, as the principal leader, an apprentice dockworker with a growing obsession for justice, interjected that the whole group had been acquainted with bitter payroll cuts; his eldest grandchild, seeing his grandfather’s desperate looks, knew the time had come to deploy their equalizer, a rickety getaway car gunned by a crackerjack driver, even if it meant an indecent sacrifice to reverse their fate. The dramatic kidnap unfolded without occlusion, as the griffin-like vampire, in a dark wetsuit with appley juices on his tonsils from earlier brownies, gaffed the primary target and multiplied the chaos, his cheeky presence a lampblack smear against the dark sky, while the lightweight greyhound, a true valkyrie, wearing fake handcuffs and a placard, managed to create enough thickness in the distraction for the getaway, even as the muckraked elites objected, their sanity already amortized by the umpteenth attack. In the wastelands hideout, smelling faintly of lichen and lettuce, the coalition celebrated their award, a testament to their working unity, finding beauty in their blues, while the eagle-eyed apprentice, his melancholy now softened, knew they could rejoin the fight, no longer needing to entrench themselves in the shadows, having found their answer and a path forward from the awful fantasy that had gripped their lives, allowing them to finally shed the awkward weight of injustice.
panelSentences: Under the streaked night, a vulnerable grandfather, peering through his eyeglass, watched vicariously as a hacky coalition, planning to upcycle a drainpipe by the mucky waterway, organized an overnight jubilee to demand their missing payroll from the elites who lavished themselves with omakase, mustards, and buttercup kernels, oblivious to the newer waves of unrest.

This preliminary kickoff was anything but boring, as the principal leader, an apprentice dockworker with a growing obsession for justice, interjected that the whole group had been acquainted with bitter payroll cuts; his eldest grandchild, seeing his grandfather’s desperate looks, knew the time had come to deploy their equalizer, a rickety getaway car gunned by a crackerjack driver, even if it meant an indecent sacrifice to reverse their fate.

The dramatic kidnap unfolded without occlusion, as the griffin-like vampire, in a dark wetsuit with appley juices on his tonsils from earlier brownies, gaffed the primary target and multiplied the chaos, his cheeky presence a lampblack smear against the dark sky, while the lightweight greyhound, a true valkyrie, wearing fake handcuffs and a placard, managed to create enough thickness in the distraction for the getaway, even as the muckraked elites objected, their sanity already amortized by the umpteenth attack.

In the wastelands hideout, smelling faintly of lichen and lettuce, the coalition celebrated their award, a testament to their working unity, finding beauty in their blues, while the eagle-eyed apprentice, his melancholy now softened, knew they could rejoin the fight, no longer needing to entrench themselves in the shadows, having found their answer and a path forward from the awful fantasy that had gripped their lives, allowing them to finally shed the awkward weight of injustice.
rawPanelSentences: Under the streaked night, a vulnerable grandfather, peering through his eyeglass, watched vicariously as a hacky coalition, planning to upcycle a drainpipe by the mucky waterway, organized an overnight jubilee to demand their missing payroll from the elites who lavished themselves with omakase, mustards, and buttercup kernels, oblivious to the newer waves of unrest.

This preliminary kickoff was anything but boring, as the principal leader, an apprentice dockworker with a growing obsession for justice, interjected that the whole group had been acquainted with bitter payroll cuts; his eldest grandchild, seeing his grandfather’s desperate looks, knew the time had come to deploy their equalizer, a rickety getaway car gunned by a crackerjack driver, even if it meant an indecent sacrifice to reverse their fate.

The dramatic kidnap unfolded without occlusion, as the griffin-like vampire, in a dark wetsuit with appley juices on his tonsils from earlier brownies, gaffed the primary target and multiplied the chaos, his cheeky presence a lampblack smear against the dark sky, while the lightweight greyhound, a true valkyrie, wearing fake handcuffs and a placard, managed to create enough thickness in the distraction for the getaway, even as the muckraked elites objected, their sanity already amortized by the umpteenth attack.

In the wastelands hideout, smelling faintly of lichen and lettuce, the coalition celebrated their award, a testament to their working unity, finding beauty in their blues, while the eagle-eyed apprentice, his melancholy now softened, knew they could rejoin the fight, no longer needing to entrench themselves in the shadows, having found their answer and a path forward from the awful fantasy that had gripped their lives, allowing them to finally shed the awkward weight of injustice.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist with a cybernetic left arm and glowing green dreadlocks. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A vulnerable grandfather in a trench coat, peering through a cracked eyeglass at a hacky coalition assembling by a mucky waterway under a streaked night sky, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The apprentice dockworker, a determined expression on his face, interjecting during a preliminary kickoff meeting, while his eldest grandchild subtly motions towards a rickety getaway car, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A griffin-like vampire in a dark wetsuit gaffing a target, while a lightweight greyhound-valkyrie figure, wearing fake handcuffs and a placard, creates a dramatic distraction amidst multiplying chaos, low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The coalition celebrating an award in a vast, lichen and lettuce-filled wasteland hideout, the eagle-eyed apprentice looking hopeful, a wide shot capturing the unity and beauty in their blues.

definitions.definition: Recently made, or created.
definitions.example: This is a new scratch on my car!   The band just released a new album.

definitions.definition: Additional; recently discovered.
definitions.example: We turned up some new evidence from the old files.

definitions.definition: Current or later, as opposed to former.
definitions.example: My new car is much better than my previous one, even though it is older.   We had been in our new house for five years by then.

definitions.definition: Used to distinguish something established more recently, named after something or some place previously existing.
definitions.example: New Bond Street is an extension of Bond Street.

definitions.definition: In original condition; pristine; not previously worn or used.
definitions.example: Are you going to buy a new car or a second-hand one?

definitions.definition: Refreshed, reinvigorated, reformed.
definitions.example: That shirt is dirty. Go and put on a new one.   I feel like a new person after a good night's sleep.   After the accident, I saw the world with new eyes.

definitions.definition: Newborn.
definitions.example: My sister has a new baby, and our mother is excited to finally have a grandchild.

definitions.definition: Of recent origin; having taken place recently.
definitions.example: I can't see you for a while; the pain is still too new.   Did you see the new King Lear at the theatre?

definitions.definition: Strange, unfamiliar or not previously known.
definitions.example: The idea was new to me.   I need to meet new people.

definitions.definition: Recently arrived or appeared.
definitions.example: Have you met the new guy in town?   He is the new kid at school.

definitions.definition: Inexperienced or unaccustomed at some task.
definitions.example: Don't worry that you're new at this job; you'll get better with time.   I'm new at this business.

definitions.definition: (of a period of time) Next; about to begin or recently begun.
definitions.example: We expect to grow at 10% annually in the new decade.

definitions.definition: A pipe that carries fluid which is being drained.
The type of pipe that is used to construct a drainpipe.
A type of form-fitting trousers with highly tapered legs.
To convert (waste materials, etc.) into new materials or products of higher quality and greater functionality.
To promote into a more productive or useful role.
A special group or social class of people which have a superior intellectual, social or economic status as, the elite of society.
Someone who is among the best at a certain task.
The crime, or an instance, of kidnapping.
To seize and detain a person unlawfully; sometimes for ransom.
In a way that is experienced in the imagination through the actions of another person. "she was living vicariously through her children"; by proxy.
The period between sunset and sunrise, when a location faces far away from the sun, thus when the sky is dark.
definitions.example: How do you sleep at night when you attack your kids like that!?

definitions.definition: (Astronomy) The period of darkness beginning at the end of evening astronomical twilight when the sun is 18 degrees below the horizon, and ending at the beginning of morning astronomical twilight.
(Legal) Often defined in the legal system as beginning 30 minutes after sunset, and ending 30 minutes before sunrise.
An evening or night spent at a particular activity.
definitions.example: a night on the town

definitions.definition: A night (and part of the days before and after it) spent in a place away from home, e.g. a hotel.
definitions.example: I stayed my friend's house for three nights.

definitions.definition: Nightfall.
definitions.example: from noon till night

definitions.definition: Darkness (due to it being nighttime).
definitions.example: The cat disappeared into the night.

definitions.definition: A dark blue colour, midnight blue.
A night's worth of competitions, generally one game.
To spend a night (in a place), to overnight.
Ellipsis of good night
definitions.example: Night, y'all! Thanks for a great evening!

definitions.definition: Like a hack; amateurish.
Filthy or totally dirty.
Using, or characterised by, hacks: poorly designed workarounds.
Short and interrupted, broken, jerky; hacking.
definitions.example: A hacky cough. A hacky laugh. A hacky breath. A hacky howl.

definitions.definition: A body of water, such as a river, channel or canal, that is navigable.
A conduit or watercourse, such as on the deck of a ship, to drain water.
Items delivered or completed overnight.
definitions.example: Have you looked at the overnights yet?

definitions.definition: An overnight stay, especially in a hotel or other lodging facility.
(in the plural) Viewership ratings for a television show that are published the morning after it is broadcast, and may be revised later on.
The fore part of the previous night; yesterday evening.
To stay overnight; to spend the night.
To send something for delivery the next day.
definitions.example: We can overnight you the documents for signature.

definitions.definition: Occurring between dusk and dawn.
definitions.example: The overnight ferry docked at 10AM.

definitions.definition: Complete before the next morning.
definitions.example: Don't expect overnight delivery.

definitions.definition: Of an activity or event in which participants stay overnight.
definitions.example: They sent their kids to overnight camp.

definitions.definition: During or throughout the night, especially during the evening or night just past.
definitions.example: Let it run overnight and we'll check on it in the morning.

definitions.definition: In a very short (but unspecified) amount of time.
definitions.example: The change seemed to happen overnight.

definitions.definition: A list of employees who receive salary or wages, together with the amounts due to each.
The total sum of money paid to employees.
The calculation of salaries and wages and the deduction of taxes etc.; the department in a company responsible for this.
Bribes paid to people
To place on a payroll.
Any of many herbs, of the genus Ranunculus, having yellow flowers; the crowfoot.
Any flower of the genus Narcissus; a daffodil.
Affectionate or ironic term of address.
An artificial lens, especially one of a pair.
A monocle.
An eyepiece.
The lens of the eye.
(Jewish history) A special year of emancipation supposed to be kept every fifty years, when farming was abandoned and Hebrew slaves were set free.
A 25th, 40th, 50th, 60th or 70th anniversary.
A special year (originally held every hundred years, then fifty, and then fewer) in which remission from sin could be granted as well as indulgences upon making a pilgrimage to Rome.
A time of celebration or rejoicing.
An occasion of mass manumission from slavery.
A period of fifty years; a half-century.
The core, center, or essence of an object or system.
definitions.example: the kernel of an argument

definitions.definition: The central (usually edible) part of a nut, especially once the hard shell has been removed.
A single seed or grain, especially of corn or wheat.
The stone of certain fruits, such as peaches or plums.
A small mass around which other matter is concreted; a nucleus; a concretion or hard lump in the flesh.
The central part of many computer operating systems which manages the system's resources and the communication between hardware and software components.
definitions.example: The Linux kernel is open-source.

definitions.definition: The core engine of any complex software system.
A function used to define an integral transform.
definitions.example: The Dirichlet kernel convolved with a function yields its Fourier series approximation.

definitions.definition: A set of pairs of a mapping's domain which are mapped to the same value.
For a given function (especially a linear map between vector spaces), the set of elements in the domain which are mapped to zero; (formally) given f : X → Y, the set {x ∈ X : f(x) = 0}.
definitions.example: If a function is continuous then its kernel is a closed set.

definitions.definition: For a category with zero morphisms: the equalizer of a given morphism and the zero morphism which is parallel to that given morphism.
(fuzzy set theory) The set of members of a fuzzy set that are fully included (i.e., whose grade of membership is 1).
The human clitoris.
The nucleus and electrons of an atom excluding its valence electrons.
A lean breed of dog used in hunting and racing.
A highball cocktail of vodka and grapefruit juice.
A swift steamer, especially an ocean steamer.
(of fish) To leap rapidly across the surface of the water.
To give out extremely generously; to squander.
definitions.example: They lavished money on the dinner.

definitions.definition: To give out to (somebody) extremely generously.
definitions.example: They lavished him with praise.

definitions.definition: A plant of certain species of the genus Brassica, or of related genera (especially Sinapis alba, in the family Brassicaceae, with yellow flowers, and linear seed pods).
Powder or paste made from seeds of the mustard plant, and used as a condiment or a spice.
definitions.example: When the waitress brought the food, I asked whether she had any Dijon mustard.

definitions.definition: The leaves of the mustard plant, used as a salad.
definitions.example: Mustard and cress sandwiches.

definitions.definition: Dark yellow colour, the colour of mustard.
The tomalley of a crab, which resembles the condiment.
To relinquish (a right etc.); to give up claim to; to forego.
definitions.example: If you waive the right to be silent, anything you say can be used against you in a court of law.

definitions.definition: To put aside, avoid.
To outlaw (someone).
To abandon, give up (someone or something).
To move from side to side; to sway.
To stray, wander.
To move back and forth repeatedly and somewhat loosely.
definitions.example: The flag waved in the gentle breeze.

definitions.definition: To move one’s hand back and forth (generally above the shoulders) in greeting or departure.
(metonymic) To call attention to, or give a direction or command to, by a waving motion, as of the hand; to signify by waving; to beckon; to signal; to indicate.
definitions.example: I waved goodbye from across the room.

definitions.definition: To have an undulating or wavy form.
To raise into inequalities of surface; to give an undulating form or surface to.
To produce waves to the hair.
To swing and miss at a pitch.
definitions.example: Jones waves at strike one.

definitions.definition: To cause to move back and forth repeatedly.
definitions.example: The starter waved the flag to begin the race.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To signal (someone or something) with a waving movement.
To fluctuate; to waver; to be in an unsettled state.
To move like a wave, or by floating; to waft.
A moving disturbance in the level of a body of liquid; an undulation.

definitions.definition: The ocean.
A moving disturbance in the energy level of a field.
A shape that alternatingly curves in opposite directions.
definitions.example: Her hair had a nice wave to it.

definitions.definition: Any of a number of species of moths in the geometrid subfamily Sterrhinae, which have wavy markings on the wings.
A loose back-and-forth movement, as of the hands.
definitions.example: He dismissed her with a wave of the hand.

definitions.definition: A sudden unusually large amount of something that is temporarily experienced.

definitions.definition: (by extension) One of the successive swarms of enemies sent to attack the player in certain games.
(usually "the wave") A group activity in a crowd imitating a wave going through water, where people in successive parts of the crowd stand and stretch upward, then sit.
(Japanese cuisine) Chef's choice
More or most likely to be exposed to the chance of being attacked or harmed, either physically or emotionally.

definitions.definition: More likely to be exposed to malicious programs or viruses.

definitions.definition: A father of someone's parent.
(by extension) A male forefather.
To be, or act as, a grandfather to.
The house sparrow, Passer domesticus; a small bird with a short bill, and brown, white and gray feathers.
A member of the family Passeridae, comprising small Old World songbirds.
A member of the family Emberizidae, comprising small New World songbirds.
Generically, any small, nondescript bird.
A quick-witted, lively person. Often used in the phrase cockney sparrow.
To have or obtain streaks.

definitions.definition: To run naked in public. (Contrast flash)

To inspire boredom in somebody.
To make a hole through something.

Causing boredom; unable to engage or hold the interest.

definitions.definition: Used, or designed to be used, to drill holes.

definitions.definition: Capable of penetrating; piercing.
Deployment
To prepare and arrange (usually military unit or units) for use.
definitions.example: "Deploy two units of infantry along the enemy's flank," the general ordered.

definitions.definition: To unfold, open, or otherwise become ready for use.
definitions.example: He waited tensely for his parachute to deploy.

definitions.definition: To install, test and implement a computer system or application.
definitions.example: The process for the deployment scenario includes: building a master installation of the operating system, creating its image and deploying the image onto a destination computer.

definitions.definition: (manner) In any way or manner whatever.
In any case. Used to indicate that a statement explains or supports a previous statement.
definitions.example: Anyhow, he is dead now.

definitions.definition: Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
A gold coin with a face value of ten dollars, formerly used in the United States.
A 13th-century coin minted in Europe and circulated in England as a debased sterling silver penny, outlawed under Edward I.
A score of two under par for a hole.
To score an eagle.
Offensive to good taste
Not in keeping with conventional moral values; improper, immodest or unseemly
Something complete, without any parts missing.

definitions.definition: An entirety.
Entire, undivided.
definitions.example: I ate a whole fish.

definitions.definition: Sound, uninjured, healthy.
definitions.example: He is of whole mind, but the same cannot be said about his physical state.

definitions.definition: (of food) From which none of its constituents has been removed.
definitions.example: whole wheat; whole milk

definitions.definition: As yet unworked.
In entirety; entirely; wholly.
definitions.example: I ate a fish whole!

definitions.definition: A child of someone's child.
Any of many symbiotic organisms, being associations of algae and fungi, often found as white or yellow patches on old walls, etc.
Something which gradually spreads across something else, causing damage.
A preparation for a main matter; an introduction.
Any of a series of sports events that determine the finalists
A relatively minor contest that precedes a major one, especially in boxing
In preparation for the main matter; initial, introductory, preparatory.
definitions.example: These are just the preliminary results.

definitions.definition: A distinct flavor, especially if slight.

definitions.definition: A slight trace of something; a smattering.
Heroin.
A form of fried potato; a scallop.
To get the flavor of.
To indicate or suggest something; used with of.
definitions.example: Her reckless behavior smacks of pride.

definitions.definition: To have a particular taste; used with of.
A small sailing vessel, commonly rigged as a sloop, used chiefly in the coasting and fishing trade and often called a fishing smack
A group of jellyfish.
A sharp blow; a slap. See also: spank.
The sound of a loud kiss.
A quick, sharp noise, as of the lips when suddenly separated, or of a whip.
To slap someone.
To make a smacking sound.
To strike a child (usually on the buttocks) as a form of discipline. (US spank)
To wetly separate the lips, making a noise, after tasting something or in expectation of a treat.
To kiss with a close compression of the lips, so as to make a sound when they separate.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
To fantasize (about).
To have a fancy for; to be pleased with; to like.
To imagine; to conceive mentally.
A person who works on the dock of a harbor or shipyard, usually employed to load or unload freight.
Covered in muck.
Obscene, pornographic.
definitions.example: a mucky magazine

definitions.definition: (followed by with) To furnish or give experimental knowledge of; to make (one) to know; to make familiar.
definitions.example: I think you should acquaint him with the realities of the situation.

definitions.definition: (followed by of or that) To communicate notice to; to inform; to make cognizant.
To familiarize; to accustom.
Personally known; familiar.
One who makes equal; a balancer.
A device that balances various quantities.
A goal, run, point, etc. that equalizes the score.
An electronic audio device for altering the frequencies of sound recordings.
A device, such as a bar, for operating two brakes, especially a pair of hub brakes for an automobile, with equal force.
Any device for equalizing the pull of electromagnets.
A conductor of low resistance joining the armature ends of the series field coils of dynamos connected in parallel.
A sliding panel to preserve the lateral stability of an aeroplane.
A set of arguments where two or more functions have equal values; the solution set of an equation.

A morphism whose codomain is the domain of a parallel pair of morphisms and which forms part of the limit of that parallel pair. Equivalently, a morphism which equalizes a parallel pair of morphisms in a limiting way, which is to say that any other morphism which equalizes that parallel pair factors through this limiting morphism; and moreover such factorization is unique.
A weapon, usually a blackjack or gun
Consisting of or resembling a wisp; like a slender, flexible strand or bundle.
definitions.example: The wispy smoke rose from chimneys all around town.

definitions.definition: A compact folding knife.
definitions.example: He kept a jack-knife in his pocket for various tasks.

definitions.definition: The front-dive pike, in which the body folds and unfolds.
definitions.example: It took me hundreds of dives to master even the simple jackknife

definitions.definition: A semi-trailer truck accident in which the vehicle mimics the closing of a jack-knife.
definitions.example: I have seen several jack-knives along that dangerous stretch of road.

definitions.definition: To fold in the middle, as a jackknife does.
definitions.example: The cat jackknifed in the air and landed gracefully on its feet.

definitions.definition: To cause a semi-trailer truck to fold like a jackknife in a traffic accident.
definitions.example: Before I knew what was happening, I'd jack-knifed the truck.

definitions.definition: A resampling method that applies estimators to all subsamples that each omit a single different group (possibly of a single datapoint) of the original sample to provide a sample distribution of the estimate.
Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
definitions.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.

definitions.definition: Of a person: feeble in the joints; tottering.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
The opposite of something.

definitions.definition: The act of going backwards; a reversal.
A piece of misfortune; a setback.
The tails side of a coin, or the side of a medal or badge that is opposite the obverse.

The gear setting of an automobile that makes it travel backwards.
A thrust in fencing made with a backward turn of the hand; a backhanded stroke.
A turn or fold made in bandaging, by which the direction of the bandage is changed.
To turn something around so that it faces the opposite direction or runs in the opposite sequence.

definitions.definition: To turn something inside out or upside down.
To transpose the positions of two things.
To change totally; to alter to the opposite.
To return, come back.
To turn away; to cause to depart.
To cause to return; to recall.
To revoke a law, or to change a decision into its opposite.

definitions.definition: To cause a mechanism or a vehicle to operate or move in the opposite direction to normal.
To change the direction of a reaction such that the products become the reactants and vice-versa.
To place a set of points in the reverse position
(of points) to move from the normal position to the reverse position
To overthrow; to subvert.
Opposite, contrary; going in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: The mirror showed us a reverse view of the scene.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to engines, vehicle movement etc. moving in a direction opposite to the usual direction.
definitions.example: He selected reverse gear.

definitions.definition: (of points) To be in the non-default position; to be set for the lesser-used route.
Turned upside down; greatly disturbed.
Reversed.
definitions.example: a reverse shell

definitions.definition: In which cDNA synthetization is obtained from an RNA template.
In a reverse way or direction; in reverse; upside-down.
A beef steak cut from the rib section of the cow; with the bone removed.
A beefsteak cut from the primal rib area of a bovine, with the rib bone attached to the steak cut. A bone-in rib steak.
A similar cut from another animal.
(by extension) A bone-in or boneless cut of steak, either the rib or ribeye steak.
(with “down”) To shoot someone or something, usually with a firearm.
definitions.example: He gunned down the hitmen.

definitions.definition: To speed something up.
definitions.example: He gunned the engine.

definitions.definition: To offer vigorous support to a person or cause.
definitions.example: He’s gunning for you.

definitions.definition: To seek to attack someone; to take aim at someone.
definitions.example: He's been gunning for you ever since you embarrassed him at the party.

definitions.definition: To practice fowling or hunting small game; chiefly in participial form: to go gunning.
To masturbate while observing and visible to a corrections officer.
Equipped or bedecked with guns.
definitions.example: She was armored and gunned and ready for action.

definitions.definition: A sheet of paper or cardboard with a written or printed announcement on one side for display in a public place.
A public proclamation; a manifesto or edict issued by authority.
Permission given by authority; a license.
definitions.example: to give a placard to do something

definitions.definition: An extra plate on the lower part of the breastplate or backplate of armour.
A kind of stomacher, often adorned with jewels, worn in the fifteenth century and later.
The woodwork and frame of the door of a closet etc.
To affix a placard to.
To announce with placards.
definitions.example: to placard a sale

definitions.definition: An exceptionally fine or excellent thing or person.
An expert or top-rated individual (e.g., a marksman).
Exceptionally fine or excellent; top-notch; high quality.
Expert, top-rated or high-performing.
(usually in the plural bitters) A liquid or powder, made from bitter herbs, used in mixed drinks or as a tonic.
A type of beer heavily flavored with hops.
A turn of a cable about the bitts.
To make bitter.
Having an acrid taste (usually from a basic substance).
definitions.example: The coffee tasted bitter.

definitions.definition: Harsh, piercing or stinging.
Hateful or hostile.
definitions.example: They're bitter enemies.

definitions.definition: Cynical and resentful.
definitions.example: I've been bitter ever since that defeat.

definitions.definition: Something which is not genuine, or is presented fraudulently.
A trick; a swindle.
A move meant to deceive an opposing player, used for gaining advantage for example when dribbling an opponent.
To cheat; to swindle; to steal; to rob.
To modify fraudulently, so as to make an object appear better or other than it really is
To make a counterfeit, to counterfeit, to forge, to falsify.
To make a false display of, to affect, to feign, to simulate.
definitions.example: to fake a marriage

definitions.definition: Not real; false, fraudulent.
definitions.example: Which fur coat looks fake?

definitions.definition: (of people) Insincere.
To dig or excavate a trench; to trench.
To surround or provide with a trench, especially for defense; to dig in.
definitions.example: The army entrenched its camp, or entrenched itself.

definitions.definition: To establish a substantial position in business, politics, etc.
definitions.example: Senator Cornpone was able to entrench by spending millions on each campaign.

definitions.definition: To invade; to encroach; to infringe or trespass; to enter on, and take possession of, that which belongs to another; usually followed by on or upon.
To cut in; to furrow; to make trenches in or upon.
To insert something between other things.
To say as an interruption or aside.
To interpose oneself; to intervene.
A close fitting, insulating garment usually made from neoprene or similar material designed to keep one warm during activities on or under water.
(usually in the plural) Operation; action.
Method of operation.
The incidental or subsidiary calculations performed in solving an overall problem.
definitions.example: Be sure to check your working.

definitions.definition: Fermentation.
(of bodies of water) Becoming full of a vegetable substance.
A place where work is carried on.
definitions.example: the abandoned mine workings

definitions.definition: Without planning or thinking ahead.
definitions.example: She gave an offhand speech.

definitions.definition: Careless; without sufficient thought or consideration.
definitions.example: He doesn't realise how hurtful his offhand remarks can be.

definitions.definition: Curt, abrupt, unfriendly.
definitions.example: She was quite offhand with me yesterday.

definitions.definition: Right away, immediately, without thinking about it.
definitions.example: Offhand, I'd guess that that's a yellow-bellied sapsucker.

definitions.definition: In an offhand manner.
An amorphous form of carbon made from incompletely burned organic matter; used to make pigments and inks.
To blacken using this form of carbon.
The money originally invested or loaned, on which basis interest and returns are calculated.
definitions.example: A portion of your mortgage payment goes to reduce the principal, and the rest covers interest.

definitions.definition: The chief administrator of a school.
The chief executive and chief academic officer of a university or college.
A legal person that authorizes another (the agent) to act on their behalf; or on whose behalf an agent or gestor in a negotiorum gestio acts.
definitions.example: My principal sells metal shims.

definitions.definition: The primary participant in a crime.
A partner or owner of a business.
A diapason, a type of organ stop on a pipe organ.
The construction that gives shape and strength to a roof, generally a truss of timber or iron; or, loosely, the most important member of a piece of framing.
The first two long feathers of a hawk's wing.
One of the turrets or pinnacles of waxwork and tapers with which the posts and centre of a funeral hearse were formerly crowned.
An essential point or rule; a principle.
A dancer at the highest rank within a professional dance company, particularly a ballet company.
A security principal.
Primary; most important; first level in importance.
definitions.example: Smith is the principal architect of this design.

definitions.definition: (Latinism) Of or relating to a prince; princely.
Someone or something that is awkward.
Lacking dexterity in the use of the hands, or of instruments.
definitions.example: John was awkward at performing the trick. He'll have to practice to improve.

definitions.definition: Not easily managed or effected; embarrassing.
definitions.example: An awkward silence had fallen.

definitions.definition: Lacking social skills, or uncomfortable with social interaction.
definitions.example: I'm very awkward at parties.

definitions.definition: Perverse; adverse; difficult to handle.
definitions.example: He's a right awkward chap.

definitions.definition: In a backwards direction.
The eldest child in a family, or individual in a group.
(of a player) Receiving cards from the dealer first, before any other players.
(of a hand) Having higher, or superior cards.
Of an object, concept, relationship, etc., having existed for a relatively long period of time.
definitions.example: an old abandoned building;  an old friend

definitions.definition: Having been used and thus no longer new or unused.
definitions.example: I find that an old toothbrush is good to clean the keyboard with.

definitions.definition: Having existed or lived for the specified time.
definitions.example: How old are they? She’s five years old and he's seven. We also have a young teen and a two-year-old child.

definitions.definition: (heading) Of an earlier time.
Tiresome after prolonged repetition.
definitions.example: Your constant pestering is getting old.

definitions.definition: Said of subdued colors, particularly reds, pinks and oranges, as if they had faded over time.
A grammatical intensifier, often used in describing something positive. (Mostly in idioms like good old, big old and little old, any old and some old.)
definitions.example: We're having a good old time. My next car will be a big old SUV.  My wife makes the best little old apple pie in Texas.

definitions.definition: Excessive, abundant.
To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
definitions.example: Children grow quickly.

definitions.definition: To appear or sprout.
definitions.example: A long tail began to grow from his backside.

definitions.definition: To develop, to mature.
definitions.example: As I grew throughout adolescence, I came to appreciate many things about human nature.

definitions.definition: To cause or allow something to become bigger, especially to cultivate plants.
definitions.example: Have you ever grown your hair before?

definitions.definition: To assume a condition or quality over time.
definitions.example: The boy grew wise as he matured.

definitions.definition: To become attached or fixed; to adhere.
Growth; increase
(attributive) Connected with growing
definitions.example: The growing season here begins in March.

definitions.definition: That grows.
definitions.example: Milk is good for growing children.

definitions.definition: A mythical beast having the body of a lion and the wings and head of an eagle.
A large vulture (Gyps fulvus) found in the mountainous parts of Southern Europe, North Africa, and Asia Minor, supposed to be the "eagle" of the Bible.
An English variety of apple.
A person who has just arrived from Europe.
A cadet newly arrived in British India: half English, half Indian.
A watchful guardian, especially a duenna in charge of a young woman.
An edible plant, Lactuca sativa and its close relatives, having a head of green and/or purple leaves.
The leaves of the lettuce plant, eaten as a vegetable; as a dish often mixed with other ingredients, dressing etc.
definitions.example: I’ll have a ham sandwich with lettuce and tomato.

definitions.definition: United States paper currency; dollars.
definitions.example: Twelve dollars an hour? That's a lot of lettuce!

definitions.definition: A strong yellow-green colour, like that of lettuce (also called lettuce green).
Of or relating to the drama.
Striking in appearance or effect.
Having a powerful, expressive singing voice.
(often slightly derogatory) Occurring in a relatively large but unspecified position in a sequence.
definitions.example: That's the umpteenth time I've had to tell him not to swear in front of the children.

definitions.definition: A trainee, especially in a skilled trade.

One who is bound by indentures or by legal agreement to serve a tradesperson, or other person, for a certain time, with a view to learn the art, or trade, in which his master is bound to instruct him.
One not well versed in a subject; a tyro or newbie.
To put under the care and supervision of a master, for the purpose of instruction in a trade or business.
definitions.example: He was apprenticed to a local employer.

definitions.definition: To be an apprentice to.
definitions.example: Joe apprenticed three different photographers before setting up his own studio.

definitions.definition: A compulsive or irrational preoccupation.
An unhealthy fixation.
Influence or control by evil spirits without possession.
To use a gaff, especially to land a fish.
To cheat or hoax.
To gamble.
To affix or cover with gaffer tape.
At the present time; in the current era.
The condition of being sane.
Reasonable and rational behaviour.
To search for and expose corruption or scandal, especially as a form of investigative journalism.
Black bile, formerly thought to be one of the four "cardinal humours" of animal bodies.
Great sadness or depression, especially of a thoughtful or introspective nature.
Affected with great sadness or depression.
definitions.example: Melancholy people don't talk much.

definitions.definition: To alienate (property) in mortmain.
To wipe out (a debt, liability etc.) gradually or in installments.
To even out the costs of running an algorithm over many iterations, so that high-cost iterations are much less frequent than low-cost iterations, which lowers the average running time.
Having an absolute or (more often) relative lack of light.
definitions.example: The room was too dark for reading.

definitions.definition: (of colour) Dull or deeper in hue; not bright or light.
definitions.example: my sister's hair is darker than mine;  her skin grew dark with a suntan

definitions.definition: Hidden, secret, obscure.
Without moral or spiritual light; sinister, malign.
definitions.example: a dark villain;  a dark deed

definitions.definition: Conducive to hopelessness; depressing or bleak.
definitions.example: the Great Depression was a dark time;  the film was a dark psychological thriller

definitions.definition: Lacking progress in science or the arts; said of a time period.
With emphasis placed on the unpleasant aspects of life; said of a work of fiction, a work of nonfiction presented in narrative form or a portion of either.
definitions.example: The ending of this book is rather dark.

definitions.definition: Outside, externally. This is still used in the names of some civil parishes in England, e.g. St Cuthbert Without.
Lacking something.
definitions.example: Being from a large, poor family, he learned to live without.

definitions.definition: In prostitution: without a condom being worn.
Outside of, beyond.
definitions.example: The snow was swirling without the cottage, but it was warm within.

definitions.definition: Not having, containing, characteristic of, etc.
definitions.example: It was a mistake to leave my house without a coat.

definitions.definition: Not doing or not having done something.
definitions.example: He likes to eat everything without sharing.

definitions.definition: Unless, except (introducing a clause).
The process of occluding, or something that occludes.
Anything that obstructs or closes a vessel or canal.
The alignment of the teeth when upper and lower jaws are brought together.
An occluded front.
A closure within the vocal tract that produces an oral stop or nasal stop.
The absorption of a gas or liquid by a substance such as a metal.
The blocking of the view of part of an image by another.
Yes
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
The offering of anything to a god; a consecratory rite.
The destruction or surrender of anything for the sake of something else; the devotion of something desirable to something higher, or to a calling deemed more pressing.
definitions.example: the sacrifice of one's spare time in order to volunteer

definitions.definition: Something sacrificed.
A loss of profit.
A sale at a price less than the cost or the actual value.
To offer (something) as a gift to a deity.
To give away (something valuable) to get at least a possibility of gaining something else of value (such as self-respect, trust, love, freedom, prosperity), or to avoid an even greater loss.
definitions.example: Venison has many advantages over meat from factory farms, although it still requires a hunter to sacrifice the life of a deer.

definitions.definition: To trade (a value of higher worth) for something of lesser worth in order to gain something else valued more, such as an ally or business relationship, or to avoid an even greater loss; to sell without profit to gain something other than money.
To intentionally give up (a piece) in order to improve one’s position on the board.
To advance (a runner on base) by batting the ball so it can be caught or fielded, placing the batter out, but with insufficient time to put the runner out.
To sell at a price less than the cost or actual value.
To destroy; to kill.
A fastening consisting of two metal rings, designed to go around a person's wrists, and connected by a chain or hinge.
An act or instance of multiplying.
To increase the amount, degree or number of (something).
(with by) To perform multiplication on (a number).
definitions.example: when you multiply 3 by 7, you get 21;  he multiplied several numbers

definitions.definition: To grow in number.
To breed or propagate.
To perform multiplication.
definitions.example: He had been multiplying, but it occurred to him he needed to resolve the exponents, first.

definitions.definition: To be a factor in a multiplication with (another factor).
The property of being thick (in dimension).
A measure of how thick (in dimension) something is.
definitions.example: The thickness of the Earth's crust varies from two to 70 kilometres.

definitions.definition: A layer.
definitions.example: We upholstered the seat with three thicknesses of cloth to make it more comfortable to sit on.

definitions.definition: The quality of being thick (in consistency).
definitions.example: Whip the cream until it reaches a good thickness.

definitions.definition: The property of being thick (slow to understand).
Impudent; impertinent; impertinently bold, often in a way that is regarded as endearing or amusing.
(of swimwear, underwear, etc.) tending to reveal the cheeks of the buttocks.
(Australian Aboriginal) Poisonous (of animals such as snakes), dangerous, cunning, violent, potent.
Indulged in.
A small square piece of rich cake, usually made with chocolate.
A mythical creature, a helpful elf who would secretly do people's housework for them.
A household spirit or revered ancestor.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the Eurasian genus Miletus.
A brown trout (Salmo trutta).
A tall, long-necked beer bottle, made from brown coloured glass.
A person of Arab, Indian or Hispanic descent. Also rarely used for a Native American or Pacific Islander.
A girl in the first level of girl scouts.
A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.
The paper containing the decision of arbitrators; that which is warded.
A trophy or medal; something that denotes an accomplishment, especially in a competition. A prize or honor based on merit.
(Australia, NZ, industrial relations) A negotiated minimum wage that is set for a particular trade or industry; an industrial award.
To give by sentence or judicial determination; to assign or apportion, after careful regard to the nature of the case; to adjudge
definitions.example: the arbitrators awarded damages to the complainant

definitions.definition: To determine; to make or grant an award.
To give (an award).
definitions.example: Four or five of these medals are awarded every year.

definitions.definition: To give (a person) an award.
definitions.example: He was awarded the Nobel Prize for Literature.

definitions.definition: Resembling apples, apple-like.
Of, or pertaining to, apples.
A region with no remaining resources; a desert.
definitions.example: Ten years of drought had left the area a wasteland.

definitions.definition: Any barren or uninteresting place.
definitions.example: After his experiences, he no longer found western Kansas such a wasteland.

definitions.definition: Any of the female attendants of Odin, figures said to guide fallen warriors from the battlefield to Valhalla.
definitions.example: Wagner's “Der Ring des Nibelungen” (1853) famously features valkyries.

definitions.definition: To join again; to unite after separation.
To come, or go, again into the presence of; to join the company of again.
To state in reply; -- followed by an object clause.
: To answer to a reply.
To answer, as the defendant to the plaintiff's replication.

In US patent law To re-insert a patent claim, typically after allowance of a patent application, applied to patent claims that had been withdrawn from examination under a restriction requirement, based on rejoinder .
A response or reply; something said or done in reaction to a statement or question.
definitions.example: Her answer to his proposal was a slap in the face.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem.
definitions.example: There is no simple answer to corruption.

definitions.definition: A document filed in response to a complaint, responding to each point raised in the complaint and raising counterpoints.
Either of a pair of small masses of lymphoid tissue that lie on each side of the throat and that help protect the body against infection; palatine tonsil.
Any of various small masses of lymphoid tissues, including palatine tonsils, adenoids and lingual tonsils.
The uvula.
The rising or ascent of an aircraft or rocket into flight.
definitions.example: The flight was smooth, but the takeoff was a little rough.

definitions.definition: A parody or lampoon of someone or something.
A quantification, especially of building materials.
definitions.example: I'll give you an estimate after I do the quantity takeoffs for the trusses and structural steel.

definitions.definition: The removal of sheets from the press.
The spot from which one takes off; specifically, the place from which a jumper rises in leaping.
To disagree with or oppose something or someone; (especially in a Court of Law) to raise an objection.
definitions.example: I object to the proposal to build a new airport terminal.

definitions.definition: To offer in opposition as a criminal charge or by way of accusation or reproach; to adduce as an objection or adverse reason.
To set before or against; to bring into opposition; to oppose.
A liquid from a plant, especially fruit.
definitions.example: Squeeze the orange and some juice will come out.

definitions.definition: A beverage made of juice.
definitions.example: I’d like two orange juices please.

definitions.definition: Any liquid resembling juice.
A soft drink.
Electricity.
Liquor.
Political power.
Petrol; gasoline.
Vitality.
The amount charged by a bookmaker for betting services.
Steroids.
Semen.
The vaginal lubrication that a woman naturally produces when sexually aroused.
Musical agreement between instrumentalists.
To extract the juice from something.
To energize or stimulate something.
A mythological undead creature said to feed on the blood of the living.
A person with the medical condition systemic lupus erythematosus, colloquially known as vampirism, with effects such as photosensitivity and brownish-red stained teeth.
A blood-sucking bat; vampire bat (Desmodus rotundus)
A person who drains one's time, energy, money, etc.
A vamp: a seductive woman who exploits men.
A medical technician who works with patients' blood.
To drain of energy or resources.
A means of escape.
The effecting of an escape.
A vacation or holiday, or the destination for one.
Pertaining to an escape, as in a vehicle or plans.
definitions.example: They'd been discussing their getaway plans for weeks.

definitions.definition: One's appearance or attractiveness.
definitions.example: His charm and good looks accounted for much of his popularity in the polls.

definitions.definition: A particular weight class, or member of such, as prescribed by the rules, between that of the heavier welterweight and the lighter featherweight. See Wikipedia for the specifics of each sport.
A particular weight category as prescribed by the rules, separate from an open or heavyweight class.
A competitive weight division as prescribed by the rules, between the heavier middleweight and the lighter featherweight.
One of little consequence or ability.
A person who cannot handle their drink; one who gets drunk on very little alcohol.
A political candidate with little chance of winning
Lacking in earnestness, ability, or profundity
Having less than average weight
Lacking in strength
Having a small footprint or performance impact
A place to hide.
definitions.example: They'll never find us in this makeshift hideout we've got in the woods.

definitions.definition: A hidden headquarters or place to return to.
The quality of being (especially visually) attractive, pleasing, fine or good-looking; comeliness.
definitions.example: 1988, "… beauty and recollection, like danger, glamour, greed, hunger- everything but disappointment and desire- were concepts belonging to other people.” -Second Son, Robert Ferro

definitions.definition: Someone who is beautiful.
definitions.example: Brigitte Bardot was a renowned beauty.

definitions.definition: Something that is particularly good or pleasing.
definitions.example: What a goal! That was a real beauty!

definitions.definition: An excellent or egregious example of something.
(with the definite article) The excellence or genius of a scheme or decision.
definitions.example: The beauty of the deal is it costs nothing!

definitions.definition: A beauty quark (now called bottom quark).
Beauty treatment; cosmetology.
Prevailing style or taste; rage; fashion.
(in the plural) Beautiful passages or extracts of poetry.
To make beautiful.
Of high quality, well done.
definitions.example: He made a beauty pass through the neutral zone.

definitions.definition: Thanks!
Cool!
definitions.example: It's the long weekend. Beauty!

definitions.definition: The colour of the clear sky or the deep sea, between green and violet in the visible spectrum, and one of the primary additive colours for transmitted light; the colour obtained by subtracting red and green from white light using magenta and cyan filters; or any colour resembling this.
A blue dye or pigment.
Any of several processes to protect metal against rust.
Blue clothing
definitions.example: The boys in blue marched to the pipers.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) A blue uniform. See blues.
A member of law enforcement
The sky, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: His request for leave came out of the blue.

definitions.definition: The ocean; deep waters.
The far distance; a remote or distant place.
Anything blue, especially to distinguish it from similar objects differing only in color.
A dog or cat with a slaty gray coat.
One of the colour balls used in snooker, with a value of five points.
Any of the butterflies of the subfamily Polyommatinae in the family Lycaenidae, most of which have blue on their wings.
A bluefish.
An argument.
A liquid with an intense blue colour, added to a laundry wash to prevent yellowing of white clothes.
A type of firecracker.
A bluestocking.
One of the three color charges for quarks.
